title: "ArchRInstall&Attempt1"
author: "KQ"
date: "2024-04-04"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Install Packages and Dependencies
```{r}
#if (!requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("devtools")
```
```{r}
#if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")

#BiocManager::install("chromVAR")

```
```{r}
#devtools::install_github("GreenleafLab/ArchR", ref="master", repos = BiocManager::repositories())
```
```{r}
# install.packages("Matrix")
# Specify the version you want
#version <- "1.6-3" #THIS DID NOT WORK, niether did 1.6-1, 1.6-2
# Install that version from CRAN
#devtools::install_version("Matrix", version = version)
```
```{r}
#IRLBA MUST BE INSTALLED AFTER MATRIX 
# 
# remove.packages("Matrix")
# remove.packages("irlba")
```
```{r} 
# #https://github.com/cole-trapnell-lab/monocle3/issues/690
# install.packages("Matrix",type="source", dependencies=T)
# ```
# ```{r}
# install.packages("irlba",type="source", dependencies=T)
```
```{r}
# Get a data frame of installed packages
#installed_packages <- as.data.frame(installed.packages()[, c("Package", "Version")])

# Print the data frame
#print(installed_packages, row.names = FALSE)
```
```{r}
#if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
#    install.packages("BiocManager")
#BiocManager::install("Rsamtools")
```

##Creating the ArchR Project
#Launch ArchR
```{r}
library(ArchR)
#ArchR::installExtraPackages()
library(Rsamtools)
library(irlba)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

library(pheatmap)
library(ggplot2)
library(hexbin)
library(magick)
library(grid)
library(ggseqlogo)

library(ggimage)
library(TFBSTools)
library(grid)
```

```{r}
set.seed(1)
```

#Read Reference Genome
```{r}
addArchRGenome("mm10")
```
#Input Files
```{r}

setwd("/Users/queitsch/Documents/AdeyLab/1_Hybrid_Fusion/ATAC_Data/ArchRInputData")

```

```{r}
# List of BAM files
bamfiles <- c("221025_iCell8_ATAC.bbrd.q10.filt.bam")
```

```{r}
# Prepend the directory path to each file name
#baminput <- paste0(getwd(), "/", bamfiles)
```

```{r}
PathToSampleNames <- "/Users/queitsch/Documents/AdeyLab/1_Hybrid_Fusion/ATAC_Data/ArchRInputData/240715_iCell8_ATAC.bbrd.q10.filt.samplelist.dummy.txt"

sampleNamesInput <- readLines(PathToSampleNames)
```

```{r}
# # Set the path to your .list file
# listFilePath <- "./221025_iCell8_ATAC.bbrd.q10.filt.cellIDslist.txt"
# # Read the file into R and convert it to a character vector
# validBarcodes <- readLines(listFilePath)
```
#Test size and structure of input
```{r}
# class(baminput)
# system(paste("wc -l ", baminput, sep = ""))
# 
# print(sampleNamesInput)
```
#Create ArrowFile
```{r}
ArrowFiles805 <- createArrowFiles(
  inputFiles = bamfiles,
  sampleNames = sampleNamesInput,
  gsubExpression=":.*",
  #validBarcodes = validBarcodes,
  minTSS = 4, #Dont set this too high because you can always increase later
  minFrags = 1000,
  addTileMat = TRUE,
  addGeneScoreMat = TRUE,
  #bcTag = "CB", # This is the tag that contains the cell-specific barcode information in your BAM file
  #bamFlag = list(isMinusStrand = FALSE, isProperPair = TRUE, isDuplicate = FALSE)
)
```
#Check it's a character vector
```{r}
class(ArrowFiles805)
file.exists(ArrowFiles805)
```
#Creating ArchRProject
```{r}
projHybrid805 <- ArchRProject(
  ArrowFiles = ArrowFiles805, 
  outputDirectory = "projHybrid805",
  copyArrows = TRUE #This is recommened so that if you modify the Arrow files you have an original copy for later usage.
)
```
#Save ArchRProject
```{r}
saveArchRProject(ArchRProj = projHybrid805, outputDirectory = "Save-projHybrid805", load = FALSE)
```

##Load ArchRProject

```{r}
#Shortern
proj <- projHybrid805
```

```{r}
# Load your custom annotation data
customAnnotations <- read.table("/Users/queitsch/Documents/AdeyLab/1_Hybrid_Fusion/ATAC_Data/ArchRInputData/240418_KQ_bysample.annot.csv", header = FALSE, sep = "\t")

```

```{r}
# Get cellColData
cellColData <- getCellColData(proj)
```
```{r}
# Get cell IDs from cellColData
cellColDataIDs <- rownames(cellColData)
```

```{r}
barcodes <- sapply(strsplit(rownames(cellColData), "#"), "[", 2)
```

```{r}
barcodes_df <- data.frame(barcodes = barcodes)
```

```{r}
merged_df <- merge(barcodes_df, customAnnotations, by.x = "barcodes", by.y = "V1")
```

#Adding the barcodes column to the cellColData
```{r}
# Using the $ operator
cellColData$barcodes <- barcodes
```

#Sanity Check - the first five barcodes match those from the row names!
```{r}
# Get the first five rows of cellColData
first_five_rows <- head(cellColData, 5)

# Get the row names
rownames_first_five <- rownames(first_five_rows)

# Get the barcodes (assuming 'barcodes' is the column name for barcodes)
barcodes_first_five <- first_five_rows$barcodes

# Print the row names and barcodes
print(rownames_first_five)
print(barcodes_first_five)

```

```{r}
# Assuming 'barcodes' is the name of the column in both dataframes
final_df <- merge(cellColData, merged_df[, c("barcodes", "V2")], by = "barcodes", all.x = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# Assuming merged_df is your dataframe
# Rename "V2" to "Cell Type"
#matchmerged_df <- rename(merged_df, `Cell Type` = V2)


# Rename "V2" to "Cell Type"
matchmerged_df <- merged_df %>% rename_with(~ "Cell Type", V2)
colnames(merged_df)


# Assuming 'df' is your data frame and 'barcodes' is the column with the barcodes
matchmerged_df$row_names <- paste("HybridATACExperiment#", matchmerged_df$barcodes, sep="")

# If you want to set these as actual row names in the dataframe
rownames(matchmerged_df) <- matchmerged_df$row_names

```

```{r}
# Assuming 'matchedmerged_df' is your dataframe with new columns and 'proj' is your ArchR project
# Make sure the rownames of df match the cell names in the ArchR project

# Convert dataframe to named a character
matchmerged_char <- as.character(matchmerged_df)

```

```{r}
class(matchmerged_char)
```

```{r}
proj <- addCellColData(
  ArchRProj = proj, # The ArchR project
  data = matchmerged_df$`Cell Type`,  # The new columns
  cells = matchmerged_df$row_names, #THANK YOU KEVIN 
  name = "CellType", # The name of the table in the ArchR project to which the columns will be added
  force = FALSE
)
```

#Save ArchRProject
```{r}
saveArchRProject(ArchRProj = proj, outputDirectory = "Save-projHybrid805.2", load = FALSE)
```

#Access TSS-E Score
```{r}
quantile(proj$TSSEnrichment)
```

##QC and Initial Visualization
#Log10 Unique Fragments vs. TSS
```{r}
logvsTSS <- getCellColData(proj, select = c("log10(nFrags)", "TSSEnrichment"))
logvsTSS
colnames(logvsTSS)
# Convert DFrame to data.frame
logvsTSS_df <- as.data.frame(logvsTSS)
```

```{r}
summary(logvsTSS)
```

#Visualize LogvsTSS
```{r}
# p <- ggPoint(
#     x = logvsTSS[,"log10(nFrags)"], 
#     y = logvsTSS[,"TSSEnrichment"], 
#     colorDensity = TRUE,
#     continuousSet = "sambaNight",
#     xlabel = "Log10 Unique Fragments",
#     ylabel = "TSS Enrichment",
#     xlim = c(log10(500), quantile(logvsTSS[,"log10(nFrags)"], probs = 0.99)),
#     ylim = c(0, quantile(logvsTSS[,"TSSEnrichment"], probs = 0.99))
# ) + geom_hline(yintercept = 4, lty = "dashed") + geom_vline(xintercept = 3, lty = "dashed")

#Attempt to use ggplot2 instead of ggPoint
library(ggplot2)

# p <- ggplot(logvsTSS_df, aes(x = log10(nFrags), y = TSSEnrichment)) +
#     geom_point() +
#     scale_x_continuous(limits = c(log10(500), stats::quantile(logvsTSS_df$log10(nFrags), probs = 0.99))) +
#     scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, stats::quantile(logvsTSS_df$TSSEnrichment, probs = 0.99))) +
#     geom_hline(yintercept = 4, lty = "dashed") +
#     geom_vline(xintercept = 3, lty = "dashed") +
#     labs(x = "Log10 Unique Fragments", y = "TSS Enrichment")

# Get summary statistics
summary_stats <- summary(logvsTSS_df)

# Calculate 99th percentile for each variable
quantile_nFrags <- quantile(logvsTSS_df$log10.nFrags., probs = 0.99)
quantile_TSSEnrichment <- quantile(logvsTSS_df$TSSEnrichment, probs = 0.99)

p <- ggplot(logvsTSS_df, aes(x = log10.nFrags., y = TSSEnrichment)) +
    geom_point() +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(log10(500), quantile_nFrags)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, quantile_TSSEnrichment)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 4, lty = "dashed") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 3, lty = "dashed") +
    labs(x = "Log10 Unique Fragments", y = "TSS Enrichment")

print(p)


plotPDF(p, name = "TSS-vs-Frags.pdf", ArchRProj = proj, addDOC = FALSE)
```
#Trouble Shooting ggplot 
```{r}
#any(is.na(logvsTSS_df))
#summary(logvsTSS_df)
#str(logvsTSS_df)
#This all looked good
```

#Ridge Plot for TSS
```{r}
p1 <- plotGroups(
    ArchRProj = proj, 
    groupBy = "Sample", 
    colorBy = "cellColData", 
    name = "TSSEnrichment",
    plotAs = "ridges"
   )
p1
```
#Violin Plot for TSS
```{r}
p2 <- plotGroups(
    ArchRProj = proj, 
    groupBy = "Sample", 
    colorBy = "cellColData", 
    name = "TSSEnrichment",
    plotAs = "violin",
    alpha = 0.4,
    addBoxPlot = TRUE
   )

p2
#ggsave(p2)
```
#Ridge Plot per Sample Log10 Reads
```{r}
p3 <- plotGroups(
    ArchRProj = proj, 
    groupBy = "Sample", 
    colorBy = "cellColData", 
    name = "log10(nFrags)",
    plotAs = "ridges"
   )
p3
#ggsave(p3)
```
#Violin Plot per Sample Log10 Reads
```{r}
p4 <- plotGroups(
    ArchRProj = proj, 
    groupBy = "Sample", 
    colorBy = "cellColData", 
    name = "log10(nFrags)",
    plotAs = "violin",
    alpha = 0.4,
    addBoxPlot = TRUE
   )
p4
#ggsave(p4)
```
#3.4: Size Distribution and TSS E Profiles
```{r}
p5 <- plotFragmentSizes(ArchRProj = proj)
p5
```
#3.4: ...and TSS E Profiles
```{r}
p6 <- plotTSSEnrichment(ArchRProj = proj)
p6
```
#Save ArchRProject
```{r}
saveArchRProject(ArchRProj = proj, outputDirectory = "Save-projHybrid805.2", load = FALSE)
```

```{r}
proj <- loadArchRProject(path = "Save-projHybrid805.2")
```

##Filter doublets (SKIP: Previously Done in BAM Generation)
```{r}
# projHybrid2 <- filterDoublets(projHybrid1)
# projHeme2
# projHemeTmp <- filterDoublets(projHybrid1, filterRatio = 1.5)
```

##Dimensionality Reduction w/ ArchR
```{r}
proj <- addIterativeLSI(
    ArchRProj = proj,
    useMatrix = "TileMatrix", 
    name = "IterativeLSI", 
    iterations = 2, 
    clusterParams = list( #See Seurat::FindClusters
        resolution = c(0.2), 
        sampleCells = 10000, 
        n.start = 10
    ), 
    varFeatures = 25000, 
    dimsToUse = 1:30
)
```
##Clustering*

```{r}
#install.packages("hexbin")
library(hexbin)
```


```{r}
proj <- addClusters(
    input = proj,
    reducedDims = "IterativeLSI",
    method = "Seurat",
    name = "Clusters",
    resolution = 0.8
)
```

```{r}
head(proj$Clusters)
table(proj$Clusters)
```

```{r}
cM <- confusionMatrix(paste0(proj$Clusters), paste0(proj$CellType))

```
#Clustering Heatmap
```{r} 
library(pheatmap)
cM <- cM / Matrix::rowSums(cM)
hm1 <- pheatmap::pheatmap(
    mat = as.matrix(cM),
    color = paletteContinuous("whiteBlue"),
    border_color = "black")

```
# Add UMAP
```{r}
proj <- addUMAP(
    ArchRProj = proj, 
    reducedDims = "IterativeLSI", 
    name = "UMAP", 
    nNeighbors = 30, 
    minDist = 0.5, 
    metric = "cosine"
)
```

```{r}


umap1 <- plotEmbedding(ArchRProj = proj, colorBy = "cellColData", name = "CellType", embedding = "UMAP")

umap1


```

```{r}
# Define the cluster mapping
cluster_mapping <- c(
  "C2" = "Macrophage",
  "C4" = "Tumor-cluster1",
  "C1" = "Hybrid-M",
  "C3" = "Hybrid-T",
  "C5" = "Tumor-cluster2",
  "C6" = "Tumor-cluster3"
)

# Add the new column to the ArchRProj
proj <- addCellColData(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  data = cluster_mapping[proj$Clusters],
  name = "UpdatedClusters",
  cells = getCellNames(proj),
  force = TRUE
)

customColors <- c(
  "Macrophage" = "cadetblue3",
  "Tumor-cluster1" = "grey45",
  "Tumor-cluster2" = "grey45",
  "Tumor-cluster3" = "grey45",
  "Hybrid-M" = "blue4",
  "Hybrid-T" = "red3")
# Ensure UpdatedClusters is a factor with levels matching customColors
proj$UpdatedClusters <- factor(proj$UpdatedClusters, levels = names(customColors))
# Check the levels of UpdatedClusters
levels(proj$UpdatedClusters)

#umap2 <- plotEmbedding(ArchRProj = proj, colorBy = "cellColData", name = "CellType", embedding = "UMAP")
umap3 <- plotEmbedding(ArchRProj = proj, colorBy = "cellColData", name = "UpdatedClusters", embedding = "UMAP", colorList = customColors)
#umap2
umap3

# Extract UMAP coordinates and cluster information
umap_df <- as.data.frame(getEmbedding(proj, embedding = "UMAP"))
umap_df$UpdatedClusters <- proj$UpdatedClusters

# Rename columns for easier plotting
colnames(umap_df) <- c("UMAP_Dimension_1", "UMAP_Dimension_2", "UpdatedClusters")

# Create UMAP plot using ggplot2
ggplot(umap_df, aes(x = UMAP_Dimension_1, y = UMAP_Dimension_2, color = UpdatedClusters)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = customColors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "UMAP of IterativeLSI colored by Updated Clusters")
```

# Simplifying Clusters
```{r}
#Confirming Current Cluster Assignments
table(proj$UpdatedClusters)
# Combining Tumor Clusters
newClusters <- as.character(proj$UpdatedClusters)
newClusters[newClusters %in% c("Tumor-cluster1", "Tumor-cluster2", "Tumor-cluster3")] <- "Tumor"
proj$Clusters_TuMerged <- newClusters
#Confirming Tumor Merged Cluster Assignments
table(proj$Clusters_TuMerged)
```
#Remaking UMAP w/ Merged Tumor Clusters
```{r}
customColors <- c(
  "Macrophage" = "cadetblue3",
  "Tumor" = "grey45",
  "Hybrid-M" = "blue4",
  "Hybrid-T" = "red3")
# Ensure UpdatedClusters is a factor with levels matching customColors
proj$Clusters_TuMerged <- factor(proj$Clusters_TuMerged, levels = names(customColors))
# Check the levels of UpdatedClusters
levels(proj$Clusters_TuMerged)

#umap2 <- plotEmbedding(ArchRProj = proj, colorBy = "cellColData", name = "CellType", embedding = "UMAP")
umap3 <- plotEmbedding(ArchRProj = proj, colorBy = "cellColData", name = "Clusters_TuMerged", embedding = "UMAP", colorList = customColors)
#umap2
umap3

# Extract UMAP coordinates and cluster information
umap_df <- as.data.frame(getEmbedding(proj, embedding = "UMAP"))
umap_df$Clusters_TuMerged <- proj$Clusters_TuMerged

# Rename columns for easier plotting
colnames(umap_df) <- c("UMAP_Dimension_1", "UMAP_Dimension_2", "Clusters_TuMerged")

# Create UMAP plot using ggplot2
ggplot(umap_df, aes(x = UMAP_Dimension_1, y = UMAP_Dimension_2, color = Clusters_TuMerged)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = customColors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "UMAP of IterativeLSI colored by Simplified Clusters")

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(umap_df, aes(x = UMAP_Dimension_1, y = UMAP_Dimension_2, color = Clusters_TuMerged)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = customColors) +
  coord_fixed() +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),  # Makes both axes black
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"), # Optional: makes tick marks black too
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),  # Optional: makes axis labels black
    axis.title = element_text(color = "black")  # Optional: makes axis titles black
  ) +
  labs(title = "UMAP of IterativeLSI with Black Axes")


```
# TSNE
```{r}
proj <- addTSNE(
    ArchRProj = proj, 
    reducedDims = "IterativeLSI", 
    name = "TSNE", 
    perplexity = 30
)
```
```{r}

tsne1 <- plotEmbedding(ArchRProj = proj, colorBy = "cellColData", name = "CellType", embedding = "TSNE")

tsne1
```

# Save ArchRProject
```{r}
saveArchRProject(ArchRProj = proj, outputDirectory = "Save-projHybrid805.2", load = FALSE)
```

##Marker Genes

# Load ArhcRProject if Needed
```{r}
proj <- loadArchRProject(path = "Save-projHybrid805.2")
```

```{r}
markersGS <- getMarkerFeatures(
    ArchRProj = proj, 
    useMatrix = "GeneScoreMatrix", 
    groupBy = "Clusters",
    bias = c("TSSEnrichment", "log10(nFrags)"),
    testMethod = "wilcoxon"
)

markerList <- getMarkers(markersGS, cutOff = "FDR <= 0.01 & Log2FC >= 1.25")
markerList$C5
```

```{r}
#install.packages("magick")
#library(magick)
```

```{r}
markerGenes  <- c(
    "CCR5", "CD14", "CD68", #Macrophage
    #MC38
    "RUNX1", "RUNX2", "NR1H3", "NTRK1", "BICDL2", "MIR99B", "Mtss1l", "Gng13" #Hybrid Fusion
)

heatmapGS <- plotMarkerHeatmap(
  seMarker = markersGS, 
  cutOff = "FDR <= 0.01 & Log2FC >= 1.25", 
  labelMarkers = markerGenes,
  transpose = TRUE
)
heatmapGS
```

```{r}
ComplexHeatmap::draw(heatmapGS, heatmap_legend_side = "bot", annotation_legend_side = "bot")

```

```{r}
plotPDF(heatmapGS, name = "GeneScores-Marker-Heatmap", width = 8, height = 6, ArchRProj = proj, addDOC = FALSE)
```

```{r}
umap.marker <- plotEmbedding(
    ArchRProj = proj, 
    colorBy = "GeneScoreMatrix", 
    name = markerGenes, 
    embedding = "UMAP",
    quantCut = c(0.01, 0.95),
    imputeWeights = NULL
)

umap.marker
```

```{r}
umap.allgenes <- lapply(umap.marker, function(x){
    x + guides(color = FALSE, fill = FALSE) + 
    theme_ArchR(baseSize = 6.5) +
    theme(plot.margin = unit(c(0, 0, 0, 0), "cm")) +
    theme(
        axis.text.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.ticks.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.text.y=element_blank(), 
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank()
    )
})
do.call(cowplot::plot_grid, c(list(ncol = 3),umap.allgenes))
```

#MAGIC
```{r}
proj <- addImputeWeights(proj)
```

```{r}
magicplot <- plotEmbedding(
    ArchRProj = proj, 
    colorBy = "GeneScoreMatrix", 
    name = markerGenes, 
    embedding = "UMAP",
    imputeWeights = getImputeWeights(proj)
)
magicplot
```

# Save ArchRProject
```{r}
saveArchRProject(ArchRProj = proj, outputDirectory = "Save-projHybrid805.2", load = FALSE)
```

#Track Plotting
```{r}
proj <- loadArchRProject(path = "Save-projHybrid805.2")
```

```{r}
trackplot <- plotBrowserTrack(
    ArchRProj = proj, 
    groupBy = "Clusters", 
    geneSymbol = markerGenes, 
    upstream = 50000,
    downstream = 50000
)
```

```{r}
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(trackplot$Runx1)
```

#Renaming the Clusters was done here... [yes so the "fresh" hybrids that were from the plates we sorted that day without expanding the GFP/RFP double positive cells are more close to macrophages. and the hybrids from 7-1-22 experiment are the ones that had been selected over time in culture and sorted for double positive RFP+GFP over time]
```{r}
# Define the cluster mapping
cluster_mapping <- c(
  "C2" = "Macrophage",
  "C4" = "Tumor-cluster1",
  "C1" = "Hybrid-M",
  "C3" = "Hybrid-T",
  "C5" = "Tumor-cluster2",
  "C6" = "Tumor-cluster3"
)

# Add the new column to the ArchRProj
proj <- addCellColData(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  data = cluster_mapping[proj$Clusters],
  name = "UpdatedClusters",
  cells = getCellNames(proj),
  force = TRUE
)

```
#Trouble Shooting Steps
```{r} 
#table(archrProj$UpdatedClusters) #worked
#head(proj$UpdatedClusters)
#table(proj$UpdatedClusters)
```

```{r}
# Define your custom color palette
customColors <- c(
  "Macrophage" = "cadetblue3",
  "Tumor-cluster1" = "grey45",
  "Tumor-cluster2" = "grey45",
  "Tumor-cluster3" = "grey45",
  "Hybrid-M" = "darkmagenta",
  "Hybrid-T" = "red3"
)
# Plot UMAP by Cell Type with custom colors
celltypecolorsnice <- plotEmbedding(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  colorBy = "cellColData",
  name = "CellType",
  embedding = "UMAP",
  colorList = customColors
)
# Plot UMAP by Updated Clusters
clusterstypecolorsnice<- plotEmbedding(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  colorBy = "cellColData",
  name = "UpdatedClusters",
  embedding = "UMAP"
)

# Display the plots
plotPDF(celltypecolorsnice, clusterstypecolorsnice, name = "UMAP_CellType_UpdatedClusters.pdf", ArchRProj = proj, addDOC = FALSE)


```

#Defining Custome Colors to Match the Manuscript that Ashley's Been Working On
```{r}
library(ArchR)
library(grid)
# Define your custom color palette
customColors <- c(
  "Macrophage" = "cadetblue3",
  "Tumor" = "grey45",
  "Hybrid-M" = "blue4", #blue4
  "Hybrid-T" = "red3")
# Consider looking at Pf4, Spi1, Thbs1
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Runx1",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
# Draw the plot
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Runx1)
dev.off()  # Close current device if open
dev.new()  # Open a new device
plot(AS1211$Runx1)
# Troubleshooting
# print(AS1211)
# str(AS1211)
#print(AS1211$Runx1)
```
#20251103:AA Supplemental Asks
```{r}
# Define the path to your folder
output_dir <- "20251103SupplementalGeneTracks"
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Epcam",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
#Check CD CD45, CD11b, CD163, CD14 and Epcam, Ecad 
#Try Cd45, Cd11b, Cd163, Cd14, Epcam, Ecad
# Draw the plot

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
# Save the plot as PDF
pdf(file.path(output_dir, "Epcam_track.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Epcam)
dev.off()
```
```{r}
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Cdh1",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
#Check CD CD45, CD11b, CD163, CD14 and Epcam, Ecad 
#Try Cd45, Cd11b, Cd163, Cd14, Epcam, Ecad
# Draw the plot

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
# Save the plot as PDF
pdf(file.path(output_dir, "Cdh1_track.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Cdh1)
dev.off()
```
#20251201:AA Supplemental Asks
```{r}
# Define the path to your folder
output_dir <- "20251201SupplementalGeneTracks"
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Cldn1",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
#Check Cldn1, Satb1, Satb2
# Draw the plot

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
# Save the plot as PDF
pdf(file.path(output_dir, "Cldn1_track.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Cldn1)
dev.off()
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Satb1",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
#Check Cldn1, Satb1, Satb2
# Draw the plot

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
# Save the plot as PDF
pdf(file.path(output_dir, "Satb1_track.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Satb1)
dev.off()
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Satb2",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
#Check Cldn1, Satb1, Satb2
# Draw the plot

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
# Save the plot as PDF
pdf(file.path(output_dir, "Satb2_track.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Satb2)
dev.off()
```
#20251201:AA Supplemental Asks 2.0
```{r}
# Just compare two groups at a time - this usually works
# First: Hybrid-T + Tumor vs Others
proj$TwoGroup <- ifelse(
  proj$Clusters_TuMerged %in% c("Hybrid-T", "Tumor"),
  "HybridT_Tumor",
  "Others"
)

# Get markers without bias correction
markers <- getMarkerFeatures(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  useMatrix = "GeneScoreMatrix",
  groupBy = "TwoGroup",
  useGroups = "HybridT_Tumor",
  bgdGroups = "Others",
  testMethod = "wilcoxon"
)

# Extract significant genes
markerList <- getMarkers(markers, cutOff = "FDR <= 0.05 & Log2FC >= 0.5")
target_genes <- markerList$HybridT_Tumor$name

print(paste("Found", length(target_genes), "candidate genes"))
print(head(target_genes, 25))
# Plot top genes
for(gene in head(target_genes, 5)) {
  p <- plotBrowserTrack(
    ArchRProj = proj,
    groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
    geneSymbol = gene,
    upstream = 50000,
    downstream = 50000,
    pal = customColors
  )
  
  pdf(paste0(output_dir, "/", gene, "_HybridT_Tumor.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)
  grid::grid.newpage()
  grid::grid.draw(p[[gene]])
  dev.off()
  
  print(paste("Plotted:", gene))
}
```
#20251201:AA Supplemental Asks Part 3.0
```{r}
# Define the path to your folder
output_dir <- "20251201SupplementalGeneTracks"
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Fosl1",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
#Check Fosl1, Col6a1, Fscn1
# Draw the plot
# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
# Save the plot as PDF
pdf(file.path(output_dir, "Fosl1_track.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Fosl1)
dev.off()
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Col6a1",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
#Check Cldn1, Satb1, Satb2
# Draw the plot

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
# Save the plot as PDF
pdf(file.path(output_dir, "Col6a1_track.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Col6a1)
dev.off()
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Fscn1",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
#Check Cldn1, Satb1, Satb2, Cyr61
# Draw the plot

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
# Save the plot as PDF
pdf(file.path(output_dir, "Fscn1_track.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Fscn1)
dev.off()
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Cyr61",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
#Check Cldn1, Satb1, Satb2, Cyr61
# Draw the plot

# Create the directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
# Save the plot as PDF
pdf(file.path(output_dir, "Cyr61_track.pdf"), width = 10, height = 8)
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Cyr61)
dev.off()
```
# Tracks for Other Genes of Interest
```{r}
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Spi1",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
# Consider looking at Pf4, Spi1, Thbs1
# Draw the plot
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Spi1)
```
```{r}
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Pf4",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
# Consider looking at Pf4, Spi1, Thbs1
# Draw the plot
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Pf4)
```
```{r}
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Thbs1",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
# Consider looking at Pf4, Spi1, Thbs1
# Draw the plot
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(AS1211$Thbs1)
```
```{r}
# Create the track plot with custom colors
AS1211 <- plotBrowserTrack(
  ArchRProj = proj,
  groupBy = "Clusters_TuMerged",
  geneSymbol = "Grn",
  upstream = 50000,
  downstream = 50000,
  pal = customColors)
